Notice Of Motion For An Award Of Attorneys Fees
Off Calendar Mar-18-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STEPHEN NARRON'S Motion For Designation as Prevailing Party and An Award Of Attorneys Fees and Costs under CCP 425.16 is Granted. No opposition filed. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/ehg

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Nov-21-2014 Off Calendar

Notice Of Joinder In Co-Defendnat Margaret L. Narron'S Spceial Motion To Strike Pltffs Verified Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-28-2014 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES - ATTY. CURTIS F. DOWLING OF THE DOWLING & MARQUEZ LLP, TEL. NO. 977-0444 EXT. 33 SPECIALLY APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT;
NO OTHER APPEARANCES ON THE MATTER.

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT STEPHEN NARRON's Joinder In Co-Defendnat Margaret L. Narron's Special Motion To Strike Plaintiff's Verified Complaint is
Granted. The Court must engage in a two-step process when ruling on an Anti-SLAPP motion under CCP sec. 425.16. "First, the court decides whether the Defendant has made a threshold showing that the cause of action is one arising from protected activity'If the court finds such a showing has been made'it then determines whether the Plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the claim." (Equilon Enterprises v. Consumer Cause Inc. (2002) 29 Cal.4th 53, 67.) The first prong is met, as the malicious prosecution claim arises from protected activity. (See Daniels v. Robbin (2010) 182 Cal.App.4th 204, 215, "The plain language of the anti-SLAPP statute dictates that every claim of malicious prosecution is a cause of action arising from protected activity because every such claim necessarily depends upon written and oral statements in a prior judicial proceeding.") The burden thus shifts to Plaintiffs to establish a probability of prevailing. Plaintiffs "must demonstrate that the complaint is both legally sufficient and supported by a sufficient prima facie showing of facts to sustain a favorable judgment." (Premier Med. Mgmt. Systems, Inc. v. Cal. Ins. Guar. Ass'n (2006) 136 Cal.App.4th 464, 476.) The elements of malicious prosecution are: (1) a favorable determination on the merits of the underlying action, (2) which was brought without probable cause, and (3) which was initiated with malice. (Contemporary Services Corp. v. Staff Pro Inc. (2007) 152 Cal.App.4th 1043, 1046.) "It is hornbook law that the plaintiff in a malicious prosecution action must plead and prove that the prior judicial proceeding of which he complains terminated in his favor." (Casa Herrera, Inc. v. Beydoun (2004) 32 Cal.4th 336, 341.) Plaintiffs have not pled or proven that CUD-14-647497 (the "Second Unlawful Detainer"), the case on which Plaintiffs' malicious prosecution claim is based, has terminated. A minute order dated February 20, 2014 in the Second Unlawful Detainer states that the Court sustained a demurrer and motion to strike with leave to amend. Although the ruling is reflected in the minutes, an Order was never filed. As such, the time for the plaintiff to amend in the Second Unlawful Detainer has yet to commence. (See CCP sec. 472b.) Further, no request for dismissal has been filed, nor has a judgment been entered. Plaintiffs therefore cannot plead or prove that the Second Unlawful Detainer terminated in their favor. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

Motion To Strike Complaint

RULING - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANT MARGARET L. NARRON'S SPECIAL Motion To Strike Complaint is Granted. The Court must engage in a two-step process when ruling on an Anti-SLAPP motion under CCP sec. 425.16. "First, the court decides whether the Defendant has made a threshold showing that the cause of action is one arising from protected activity'If the court finds such a showing has been made'it then determines whether the Plaintiff has demonstrated a probability of prevailing on the claim." The burden thus shifts to Plaintiffs to establish a probability of prevailing. The elements of malicious prosecution are: (1) a favorable determination on the merits of the underlying action, (2) which was brought without probable cause, and (3) which was initiated with malice. (Contemporary Services Corp. v. Staff Pro Inc. (2007) 152 Cal.App.4th 1043, 1046.) "It is hornbook law that the plaintiff in a malicious prosecution action must plead and prove that the prior judicial proceeding of which he complains terminated in his favor." (Casa Herrera, Inc. v. Plaintiffs have not pled or proven that CUD-14-647497 (the "Second Unlawful Detainer"), the case on which Plaintiffs' malicious prosecution claim is based, has terminated. A minute order dated February 20, 2014 in the Second Unlawful Detainer states that the Court sustained a demurrer and motion to strike with leave to amend. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter: Sheryl L. Sawyer, CSR # 5976 =302/ehg

Motion To Strike Complaint
Off Calendar Sep-24-2014 Continued To Oct-28-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Defendant Margaret L. Narron'S SPECIAL Motion To Strike Complaint is continued to October 28, 2014 based on the representation in Plaintiffs' opposition brief that "the parties jointly ask the Court to continue the hearing for at least 30 days'" (Plaintiffs' Opposition, p. 3, lines 4-5.) Any substantive opposition and reply may be filed in compliance with CCP sec. 1005 contemplating the new hearing date. The Court reminds Plaintiffs that filing a Peremptory Challenge does not increase the time period for a party to file an opposition. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert, Not reported. =(302/EHG)
